title: hemitelia parvula
intro: Hemitelia parvula, also known as the small hemitelia or small scale fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a type of non-vascular, epiphytic fern that is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. Traditionally, Hemitelia parvula has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are said to provide a range of health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
